failsafe_desc: If SMART reading is lost, fans will automatically rise to 80% to prevent blind overheating.
pred_desc: Instantly activates when the monitored global I/O breaks the configured threshold.
calib_desc: Calculates motor wear by comparing current RPM with Maximum RPM set in Settings.
env_desc: Select the sensor representing your case ambient temp (e.g. SYSTIN).
sec_2fa_desc: Add an extra layer using Authy or Google Authenticator.
wiki_term_d:
The 'Brain' of ZFS Guardian. Runs in an isolated async loop every 5 seconds. Key functions:
  • Silent Polling: Extracts temperatures using smartctl without waking up spun-down disks (power-saving friendly).
  • Thermodynamic PID: Calculates T_Max, T_Min, compensating with room ambient temperature. Supports Aggressive (responds to hottest disk), Inverse, and Delta Soft modes.
  • Inertia & Anti-Bounce: Hard drives are solid blocks of steel. The algorithm applies a 'Stabilization Lock' to prevent fans from abruptly dropping when temp dips, giving internal heat time to dissipate.
  • Permanent Fail-Safe (Panic): If the daemon loses temp readings (kernel freeze, ripped SAS/SATA cable), it triggers an emergency 80% PWM override to blindly prevent the array from frying.
wiki_zfs_d:
Stays one step ahead of the thermal sensor by talking to the low-level Linux Kernel.
  • Raw Monitoring: Computes real MB/s throughput exclusively by diffing /proc/diskstats vectors, avoiding system overhead.
  • Predictive Boost: Due to Fourier's law of thermal conduction, heat takes time to reach the outer shell. If IO exceeds your threshold (e.g., 150MB/s), it accelerates the turbines *before* the sensor registers a rise. Upon transfer completion, the burst is kept alive for a 120s cool-down grace period.
wiki_mech_d:
Hooks directly into the OS HWMON Subsystem (/sys/class/hwmon/).
  • Master Calibration: Stress tests the rotors during initialization progressively in 10% steps (up to 100%). Logs exact RPMs to the database after a 20s stabilization phase to set a mathematical Health Baseline (Logarithmic curve).
  • Optical Health check: Compare actual tachometer RPM against expected curve (Max_RPM * PWM%). If it drifts beyond ±15% tolerances, it flags bearing wear/anomalies in the UI.
  • Mechanical Jam Alarm: If it commands >40% PWM but the optical read holds at 0 RPM for 20 continuous seconds (allowing soft-start clearances), it fires a critical 'Fan Dead' alert via Telegram/Email.
wiki_hw_d:
ZFS Guardian gives you absolute dominion over the motherboard and bus adapters.
  • Persistent Identity: Identifies drives exclusively via /dev/disk/by-id/, making it entirely robust against reboots and SATA port shuffles when doing scheduled maintenance.
  • Ambient Filtering: Isolates sheer environmental sensors (e.g., MB SYSTIN). Crucial logic: 40ºC in a scorching summer is fine; 40ºC in freezing winter implies a localized rack fire.
  • Full Delegation: You determine if the software actively drives the fan ('Smart' Role), pins it to a static GUI slider ('Manual' Role), or just acts as a read-only tachometer relying entirely on BIOS/IPMI thermal curves ('Ignored').
wiki_alert_d:
Doesn't just scream when things are melting; it predicts failure before it occurs.
  • Async Daemons: Push alerts sprint in parallel threads; a crashed Telegram API or slow SMTP handshake will never bottleneck the critical main cooling loop.
  • 48H Deviation: Every drive records its own rolling SQLite average. If an isolated disk unexpectedly spikes >5ºC above its *OWN* adjusted normality, it hints at internal friction or broken bearings. Triggers 'Thermal Anomaly' warning way before breaking the 45ºC ceiling.
  • Ghost Disconnects: Controller not shouting about a dropped SAS lead? Guardian detects 'Lost Disks' the moment an assigned active drive vanishes from the live SMART polling iteration.
sys_failsafe_body: Fail-Safe protocol (80%) activated due to SMART data loss.
sys_boost_body: Boost activated due to high I/O load: {io} MB/s.
sys_boost_end_body: Predictive mode ended, temperatures under control, stabilizing fans.
js_setup_ok: ✅ Username and password successfully updated. Please login again.
js_calib_confirm: Run Deep Mechanical Test (0-100% curve)? It will take ~3.5 mins.
algo_aggr_help:
Aggressive Mode: Responds to the hottest disk.
📉 Curve: <35ºC (25%) | 35-40ºC (35%) | 40-45ºC (Ramp 50-100%).
algo_inv_help: Inverse Mode: Responds to the coldest disk. Useful to reduce noise if Delta is low.
algo_soft_help: Delta Soft Mode: Hybrid control. Adds extra power if the difference exceeds 3ºC.
tt_delta: Difference between hottest and coldest disk. Ideal < 3ºC. If higher, check local airflow.
tt_env: Motherboard base sensor selected in Settings -> Hardware.
tt_eff: Higher index means less fan effort required. If it drops suddenly, clean your dust filters.
tt_dev: Deviation from its own 48h average. If it spikes >+5ºC, internal motor friction is occurring.
tt_health: NVMe: Native flash wear. SATA SSD: Hardware specific ID (231, 202, etc). HDD: Power-On Hours heuristics.
tt_motor: Checks real-world RPM vs expected PWM curve. 'Anomaly' implies a physical jam or heavy dust blocking.
sys_fan_jam_body: Fan {fan} is not spinning (0 RPM) despite receiving movement signal.
sys_anomaly_body: Disk {disk} abnormally deviated +{dev}ºC from its weekly average!
